message:
fix(extensions): enforce READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES limit in extension host file reads

The extension host's fs_op_read function previously used fs::read(path)
which loads the entire file into memory regardless of size. For very
large files (e.g., multi-GB database dumps or binary blobs), this could
cause out-of-memory panics or excessive memory pressure.

Now opens the file and uses .take(READ_TOOL_MAX_BYTES + 1) to read at
most one byte past the 100MB limit, then checks if the read exceeded
the threshold. This matches the same safety guard already applied in
the built-in Read tool, ensuring consistent size enforcement across
both native tools and extension-hosted file operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
